text.title: GCP Architecture Pre-Deployment Checklist
text.summary: Pre-deployment checklist for GKE clusters, Cloud SQL, CDN, networking, security, and monitoring.

rule.statement: Verify: regional cluster type, minimum 3 zones, private nodes enabled, Workload Identity enabled, Binary Authorization enabled, Dataplane V2 enabled, REGULAR or STABLE release channel, Managed Prometheus enabled.
rule.rationale: Each of these settings addresses a distinct failure mode: zone failures (regional), credential leaks (Workload Identity), supply chain attacks (Binary Authorization), policy enforcement (Dataplane V2).

rule.statement: Remove default node pool after cluster creation; configure dedicated node pools with autoscaling min/max; set resource quotas per namespace.
rule.rationale: Default node pool machine type and configuration are not suitable for production workloads. Quotas prevent runaway HPA from consuming all cluster capacity.

rule.statement: Verify: availability_type = REGIONAL, private IP only (no public IP), SSL required, PITR enabled, 30+ day backup retention, Query Insights enabled, deletion protection enabled.
rule.rationale: ZONAL without these settings is the most common cause of preventable GCP database incidents — a single zone outage becomes a 30+ minute emergency with manual intervention required.

rule.statement: Verify: IAM least privilege, Secret Manager for all secrets, audit logging enabled, Cloud Armor on public load balancers, VPC Service Controls for sensitive data environments.
rule.rationale: Audit logging is required for SOC2/PCI compliance. Cloud Armor provides DDoS protection and WAF rules at the load balancer level.

rule.statement: Create CloudWatch-equivalent monitoring: uptime checks on critical endpoints, SLO definitions, alerting policies on error rate and latency, log sinks for long-term retention.
rule.rationale: SLO-based alerting reduces alert fatigue by notifying on user-visible impact rather than every anomalous metric spike.

rule.statement: Review Committed Use Discounts for steady-state GKE node pools and Cloud SQL; configure Spot VM node pools for batch workloads; review BigQuery on-demand vs slot reservations.
rule.rationale: CUDs provide 20-57% savings with 1-3 year commitment on predictable workloads. Spot VMs save 60-91% for fault-tolerant batch jobs. BigQuery slot reservations eliminate runaway query costs.

antipattern.description: Enabling Cloud SQL public IP for convenience during development and leaving it enabled in production.
antipattern.reason: Public IP Cloud SQL instances are directly exposed to the internet. Even with strong passwords, they are subject to credential stuffing and brute-force attacks continuously.
